objective: Correct Web API authority and replace invented request/auth architecture without changing accepted product UI or chess domains.

targetAudit.reason: Invents same-origin handlers, BFF ownership, cookie session, and server credential vault.
Invents production /api/ksl hosting behavior and arbitrary browser-readable override.
Non-auth persistence rules remain valid; opaque cookie session is unverified and must be removed.
Adapter model remains; API source precedence is incomplete.
Vue topology remains; cookie/same-origin and forbidden source-authority statements are replaced.
Names a forbidden authority, treats /api/ksl as a contract, absorbs URL tokens, and conflates account auth with replay auth.
Historical implementation evidence only; the proxy and browser Bearer architecture are not Web-source contracts.
Accepted UI/chess integration remains; API/auth completion claims become historical.
Preserve historical phase ledger; active Web API readiness moves to WEB_API_READINESS_MATRIX.json.
Recovered H0 security evidence is preserved as a superseded baseline; its invented server-session recommendation is not authority.
Recovered H0 planning evidence remains historical and is superseded for Web API work.
Names the forbidden chess-pgnviewer tree as API authority and omits chess-main-overseas.
Derived from the forbidden source and invented same-origin server contracts.
Product capability coverage remains; API authority fields need a correction overlay rather than historical deletion.
Preserve immutable historical execution evidence; do not treat its authority claims as current.
Preserve immutable historical development-proxy evidence.
Preserve immutable historical UI acceptance evidence; API/auth claims are superseded.
Defaults to invented /api/ksl and accepts arbitrary HTTPS or /api/* bases.
The /api/ksl proxy is development-only and has no Web-source production contract.
Keep one Axios owner, path validation, cancellation, timeout, and error normalization; remove generic browser Bearer and cookie assumptions.
Speculative response aliases, empty openid, and URL-session absorption are not authoritative.
Indefinite localStorage tokens and legacy compatibility keys violate minimum-state ownership.
Keep scoped private cleanup and local logout; remove route-query absorption and add deterministic auth-loss ownership.
Remove token/query absorption while preserving the single Router owner and canonical routes.
Use project repositories with endpoint-owned request schemas and confirmed payload fields; disable blocked replay.
Recursive envelope search, speculative aliases, and synthesized replay success are not field-level contracts.
Keep domain intent; remove fields that cannot be mapped from confirmed DTOs.
Keep one QueryClient and private cleanup; type/normalize keys and deterministic retry/privacy.
Keep accepted UI; connect to strict repository and truthful unavailable/refresh states.
Keep route/UI state; use strict group/round/pairing repositories and preserve independent errors.
Keep read-only display; polling must stop on deterministic unavailable state.
Render a truthful blocked state until the non-openid Web login contract is verified.
Keep cancellation/private cleanup; remove incorrect account-auth replay request and expose blocked contract.
Sanitized handoff and explicit unavailable resolution are truthful; no blocked API is called.
